messages.content:
Can you please show me how to write the VBA code and how to create the form for the following event:
Every time I enter a number (not a letter) into B:B pop up a Form that
Has entries for Start Time & End Time.
After entering the values in the Form and I click OK,
the Start time entry is inserted into column 'AD' on the same row as my active cell  in column B
The End Time entry is inserted into column 'AE' on the same row as my active cell in column B.

Sure! Here's an example of the VBA code and steps to create the form for the mentioned event:

Step 1: Insert a new module in your VBA editor by right-clicking on your workbook VBAProject in the Project Explorer, and selecting Insert -> Module.

Step 2: In the newly created module, enter the following code:

```vba
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Check if the changed cell is in column B and the input is a number
    If Target.Column = 2 And IsNumeric(Target.Value) Then
        ' Show the form
        ShowTimeForm Target
    End If
End Sub

Sub ShowTimeForm(ByVal rng As Range)
    ' Create a UserForm and add necessary controls
    Dim TimeForm As UserForm
    Set TimeForm = ThisWorkbook.VBProject.VBComponents.Add(vbext_ct_MSForm).Designer

    With TimeForm
        ' Add labels
        .Controls.Add("Forms.Label.1", "StartTimeLabel", True)
        .Controls("StartTimeLabel").Caption = "Start Time"
        .Controls("StartTimeLabel").Top = 10
        .Controls("StartTimeLabel").Left = 10

        .Controls.Add("Forms.Label.1", "EndTimeLabel", True)
        .Controls("EndTimeLabel").Caption = "End Time"
        .Controls("EndTimeLabel").Top = 40
        .Controls("EndTimeLabel").Left = 10

        ' Add text boxes
        .Controls.Add("Forms.TextBox.1", "StartTimeTextBox", True)
        .Controls("StartTimeTextBox").Top = 10
        .Controls("StartTimeTextBox").Left = 100

        .Controls.Add("Forms.TextBox.1", "EndTimeTextBox", True)
        .Controls("EndTimeTextBox").Top = 40
        .Controls("EndTimeTextBox").Left = 100

        ' Add OK button
        .Controls.Add("Forms.CommandButton.1", "OKButton", True)
        .Controls("OKButton").Caption = "OK"
        .Controls("OKButton").Top = 70
        .Controls("OKButton").Left = 10
        .Controls("OKButton").Width = 75

        ' Add Cancel button
        .Controls.Add("Forms.CommandButton.1", "CancelButton", True)
        .Controls("CancelButton").Caption = "Cancel"
        .Controls("CancelButton").Top = 70
        .Controls("CancelButton").Left = 90
        .Controls("CancelButton").Width = 75

        ' Show the form as modeless
        .Show vbModeless
    End With
End Sub

Private Sub TimeForm_OKButton_Click()
    ' Get the active cell
    Dim activeCell As Range
    Set activeCell = ActiveCell

    ' Update the start and end times in columns AD and AE
    activeCell.Offset(, 29).Value = TimeForm.StartTimeTextBox.Value
    activeCell.Offset(, 30).Value = TimeForm.EndTimeTextBox.Value

    ' Close the form
    TimeForm.Hide
End Sub

Private Sub TimeForm_CancelButton_Click()
    ' Close the form
    TimeForm.Hide
End Sub
```

Step 3: Next, go back to your worksheet and right-click on the sheet's name in the Project Explorer. Select View Code.

Step 4: In the worksheet code module, enter the following code:

```vba
Option Explicit

Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Hide the form when the selection changes
    If Not TimeForm Is Nothing Then
        TimeForm.Hide
    End If
End Sub
```

That's it! Now, whenever you enter a number in column B, a form will pop up asking for the start and end times. After entering the values and clicking OK, the start and end times will be inserted into columns AD and AE, respectively, on the same row as the active cell in column B.

Can you show me a different method. I have created a form called 'TimeForm1' with two text box entries for the Start time and End time called 'StartTime' & 'EndTime' and a button called 'StartEndButton1'

Certainly! Here's an alternative method using the provided form and button names:

Step 1: Insert a new module in your VBA editor.

Step 2: In the newly created module, enter the following code:

```vba
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Hide the form when the selection changes
    If Not TimeForm1 Is Nothing Then
        TimeForm1.Hide
    End If
End Sub

Sub ShowTimeForm(ByVal rng As Range)
    ' Show the form
    TimeForm1.Show vbModal
End Sub

Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Check if the changed cell is in column B and the input is a number
    If Target.Column = 2 And IsNumeric(Target.Value) Then
        ' Show the form
        ShowTimeForm Target
    End If
End Sub

Private Sub StartEndButton1_Click()
    ' Get the active cell
    Dim activeCell As Range
    Set activeCell = ActiveCell

    ' Update the start and end times in columns AD and AE
    activeCell.Offset(, 29).Value = TimeForm1.StartTime.Value
    activeCell.Offset(, 30).Value = TimeForm1.EndTime.Value

    ' Hide the form
    TimeForm1.Hide
End Sub
```

Step 3: In the form's code module, enter the following code:

```vba
Private Sub UserForm_QueryClose(Cancel As Integer, CloseMode As Integer)
    ' Cancel the close event to avoid closing the form unintentionally
    If CloseMode = 0 Then
        Cancel = True
        Me.Hide
    End If
End Sub
```

That's it! Now, when you enter a number in column B, the form 'TimeForm1' will pop up asking for the start and end times. After entering the values and clicking the button 'StartEndButton1', the start and end times will be inserted into columns AD and AE, respectively, on the same row as the active cell in column B.
When I click the Form Button, I get an error 'Object variable or With block variable not set' on the line of code in the form button 'activeCell.Offset(, 29).Value = TimeForm1.StartTime.Value'

Apologies for the confusion. It seems that the active cell is not correctly passed to the form. 

To fix the error, we need to modify the code as follows:

Step 1: In the module containing the worksheet event code, update the `ShowTimeForm` sub to pass the ActiveCell to the form as an argument:

```vba
Sub ShowTimeForm(ByVal rng As Range)
    ' Show the form and pass the active cell as an argument
    TimeForm1.Show rng
End Sub
```

Step 2: In the form's code module, add a new property variable to store the active cell:

```vba
Private ActiveCellRange As Range
```

Step 3: In the form's code module, update the `UserForm_Initialize` event to receive the active cell argument and assign it to the property:

```vba
Private Sub UserForm_Initialize()
    ' Assign the active cell to the property variable
    Set ActiveCellRange = ActiveCell
End Sub
```

Step 4: In the form button's click event, update the code to use the property variable for the active cell:

```vba
Private Sub StartEndButton1_Click()
    ' Update the start and end times in columns AD and AE using the stored active cell
    ActiveCellRange.Offset(, 29).Value = StartTime.Value
    ActiveCellRange.Offset(, 30).Value = EndTime.Value

    ' Hide the form
    Me.Hide
End Sub
```

That should resolve the error and properly insert the start and end times into columns AD and AE on the same row as the active cell in column B.
The code completed, but the values are in the row below

Apologies for the confusion. To ensure that the values are inserted into the same row as the active cell in column B, we need to adjust the code further.

Update the code in the form button `StartEndButton1_Click()` event as follows:

```vba
Private Sub StartEndButton1_Click()
    ' Get the active cell row
    Dim activeRow As Long
    activeRow = ActiveCell.Row

    ' Update the start and end times in columns AD and AE on the same row as the active cell
    With ActiveSheet
        .Cells(activeRow, "AD").Value = StartTime.Value
        .Cells(activeRow, "AE").Value = EndTime.Value
    End With

    ' Hide the form
    Me.Hide
End Sub
```

By using `activeRow` variable to get the row number of the active cell, the start and end times will be inserted correctly into columns AD and AE on the same row.
It is still putting the values in the row below

Apologies for the confusion once again. Let's modify the code to ensure the values are inserted into the correct row.

Update the code in the form button `StartEndButton1_Click()` event as follows:

```vba
Private Sub StartEndButton1_Click()
    ' Get the active cell range
    Dim activeCellRange As Range
    Set activeCellRange = ActiveCell

    ' Get the row of the active cell
    Dim activeRow As Long
    activeRow = activeCellRange.Row

    ' Update the start and end times in columns AD and AE on the same row as the active cell
    With activeCellRange.Worksheet
        .Cells(activeRow, "AD").Value = StartTime.Value
        .Cells(activeRow, "AE").Value = EndTime.Value
    End With

    ' Hide the form
    Me.Hide
End Sub
```

By assigning the `ActiveCell` directly to the `activeCellRange` variable, we can ensure that the start and end times are inserted into columns AD and AE on the correct row.

Please give it a try and let me know if it works as expected.

It is still putting the values in the row below. I believe the problem is caused by the following; when I enter a number in column B and press enter to register the change, the selection immediately drops to the cell below. When the form opens, it registers the cell cell below my entry as the active range.

Apologies for the misunderstanding. You are correct in identifying the issue. When you press Enter after entering a value in column B, the selection automatically moves to the cell below, making that cell the active selection.

To address this issue and ensure that the form captures the correct active cell, we can modify the code as follows:

Step 1: In the module containing the worksheet event code, update the `Worksheet_Change` event to delay the execution of the `ShowTimeForm` sub using the `Application.OnTime` method:

```vba
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Check if the changed cell is in column B and the input is a number
    If Target.Column = 2 And IsNumeric(Target.Value) Then
        ' Delay the execution of the ShowTimeForm sub to ensure the active cell is captured correctly
        Application.OnTime Now + TimeValue("00:00:01"), "ShowTimeForm", Target
    End If
End Sub
```

Step 2: In the module containing the worksheet event code, add a new module level variable to store the range passed to the `ShowTimeForm` sub:

```vba
Private activeCellRange As Range
```

Step 3: In the module containing the worksheet event code, update the `ShowTimeForm` sub to store the active cell range in the module level variable and display the form:

```vba
Sub ShowTimeForm(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Store the active cell range in the module level variable
    Set activeCellRange = Target

    ' Show the form
    TimeForm1.Show vbModal
End Sub
```

Step 4: In the form's code module, update the `StartEndButton1_Click()` event to use the stored range:

```vba
Private Sub StartEndButton1_Click()
    ' Update the start and end times in columns AD and AE using the stored active cell range
    With activeCellRange.Worksheet
        .Cells(activeCellRange.Row, "AD").Value = StartTime.Value
        .Cells(activeCellRange.Row, "AE").Value = EndTime.Value
    End With

    ' Hide the form
    Me.Hide
End Sub
```

With these changes, the form will now correctly capture the active cell range, even if the selection moves to the cell below after pressing Enter.

Please try this updated code and let me know if it resolves the issue.
I am getting an error 'Argument not optional' on '.OnTime' in the line 'Application.OnTime Now + TimeValue("00:00:01")'

Apologies for the confusion. The `Application.OnTime` method doesn't work with the `'Worksheet_Change'` event. We need to use a different approach to handle this scenario.

Instead of using the `'Worksheet_Change'` event, we can use the `'Worksheet_SelectionChange'` event to trigger the form when a valid number is entered in column B. 

Please follow the updated steps below:

Step 1: In the module containing the worksheet event code, remove the existing `'Worksheet_Change'` event code.

Step 2: In the module containing the worksheet event code, add the following code for the `'Worksheet_SelectionChange'` event:

```vba
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Check if the changed cell is in column B and the input is a number
    If Target.Column = 2 And IsNumeric(Target.Value) Then
        ' Show the form
        ShowTimeForm Target
    End If
End Sub
```

Step 3: In the form's module, update the `StartEndButton1_Click()` event as follows:

```vba
Private Sub StartEndButton1_Click()
    ' Get the active cell row
    Dim activeRow As Long
    activeRow = Application.Caller.Row

    ' Update the start and end times in columns AD and AE on the same row as the active cell
    With ActiveSheet
        .Cells(activeRow, "AD").Value = StartTime.Value
        .Cells(activeRow, "AE").Value = EndTime.Value
    End With

    ' Hide the form
    Me.Hide
End Sub
```

Step 4: In the form's module, add the following code to handle the form's visibility and prevent it from closing unintentionally:

```vba
Private Sub UserForm_QueryClose(Cancel As Integer, CloseMode As Integer)
    ' Cancel the close event to avoid closing the form unintentionally
    If CloseMode = 0 Then
        Cancel = True
        Me.Hide
    End If
End Sub
```

Please make these modifications and let me know if it resolves the problem.

I am getting an error 'Object required' on the line 'Set activeCellRange = Target' in the event: Sub ShowTimeForm(ByVal rng As Range)
    ' Store the active cell range in the module level variable
    Set activeCellRange = Target
    ' Show the form
    TimeForm1.Show vbModal
End Sub

Apologies for the confusion. To resolve the "Object required" error, we need to define the `activeCellRange` variable as a module-level variable at the top of the module.

Please add the following line at the top of the module containing the worksheet event code:

```vba
Dim activeCellRange As Range
```

This will declare the `activeCellRange` variable at the module level, allowing it to be accessed by multiple procedures within the module.

Once you add this line at the top of the module, the error should be resolved.

Please give it a try and let me know if you encounter any further issues.
I am still getting the Object required error

I apologize for the confusion. It seems that there is an issue with the approach of capturing the active cell when using the `'Worksheet_SelectionChange'` event. To address this, we can use a different approach by capturing the active cell directly within the `'StartEndButton1_Click()'` event.

Please use the following updated code:

Step 1: In the worksheet module, remove the existing `'Worksheet_SelectionChange'` event code.

Step 2: In the form's module, update the `'StartEndButton1_Click()'` event as follows:

```vba
Private Sub StartEndButton1_Click()
    ' Get the active cell row
    Dim activeRow As Long
    activeRow = Application.Caller.Row

    ' Update the start and end times in columns AD and AE on the same row as the active cell in column B
    With ActiveSheet
        .Cells(activeRow, "AD").Value = TimeForm1.StartTime.Value
        .Cells(activeRow, "AE").Value = TimeForm1.EndTime.Value
    End With

    ' Hide the form
    Me.Hide
End Sub
```

Step 3: In the form's module, remove the `'UserForm_QueryClose()'` event code.

Step 4: In the worksheet module, update the `'Worksheet_SelectionChange'` event as follows:

```vba
Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
    ' Check if the changed cell is in column B and the input is a number
    If Target.Column = 2 And IsNumeric(Target.Value) Then
        ' Show the form
        TimeForm1.Show vbModal
    End If
End Sub
```

By directly accessing the Start and End time values from the `TimeForm1` form, and displaying the form directly within the `'Worksheet_SelectionChange'` event, we can avoid the issue of capturing the active cell.

Please try this updated code, and let me know if you encounter any further issues.
